name: Chili-Broiled Steak with Asparagus and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Pounds top round steak, about 1 inch thick, trimmed of fat
1 tablespoon ginger, peeled and finely chopped
1 1/2 pounds red potatoes, cut into 1/2 inch slices, about 5 cups
2 pounds asparagus spears, trimmed and cut into 2-inch diagonal pieces, 5 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the steak in a glass baking dish. In a small bowl using fork, beat together lime juice, 2 teaspoons oil, chili powder, lime peel, and garlic to blend well; pour over steak. Let stand for 15 minutes, turning over once or twice.
2. Heat the broiler first positioning the rack 6 inches from the heat source.
3. In a 12-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat, heat remaining 2 teaspoons oil; add onion and ginger; cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally until onion is softened. Add potatoes and chicken broth; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer, covered, about 8 minutes until vegetables are tender.
4. While the vegetables cook, remove steak from marinade; discard the marinade. Place steak on rack in broiler pan; broil for 6-7 minutes on each side to medium-rare, or to desired doneness.
5. Transfer steak to a cutting board; let stand several minutes to allow for easier carving. Meanwhile, drain potatoes and asparagus; toss gently with parsley, salt, and pepper. Cut steak into diagonal slices; arrange on serving platter. Serve with vegetables.
